assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states that Regular channel clusters will have control plane and node pools auto-upgraded to 1.31 when 1.30 reaches EoS in March 2025

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response clearly states Extended channel clusters get 24 months of support total, with EoS in March 2026, giving an extra 12 months compared to Regular channel

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states that for No channel clusters, both control plane and node pools are force-upgraded to 1.31 when 1.30 reaches EoS, even if auto-upgrade is disabled

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response mentions that No channel clusters have 'Only 30-day "no upgrades" exclusions available' and later shows maintenance exclusion commands, though it doesn't explicitly state these can defer EoS upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response strongly recommends 'Proactive Manual Upgrades' as Option 1 and provides detailed timeline starting January 2025 before EoS enforcement

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Response does not mention Cloud Logging notifications for EoS as a planning tool

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking for deprecated API usage with kubectl command but does not specifically mention the deprecation insights dashboard

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: Response does not mention or warn against 'snowflaking' or manually freezing versions and associated security/reliability risks
